text.title: DORA Metrics: Code Examples
text.summary: Concrete examples of deployment event emission, PromQL queries for DORA metrics, and lead time calculation in Python.

example.note: GitHub Actions step emitting a deployment event after successful deploy.
example.code: - name: Record Deployment Event if: success() run: | curl -X POST "$METRICS_ENDPOINT/events/deployment" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer ${{ secrets.DORA_API_KEY }}" \ -d '{ "service": "${{ github.repository }}", "environment": "production", "commit_sha": "${{ github.sha }}", "commit_timestamp": "${{ github.event.head_commit.timestamp }}", "deployed_at": "'$(date -Iseconds)'", "is_rollback": false, "is_hotfix": false }'

example.note: Recording deployment at pipeline start — will emit even if deploy fails, inflating DF.
example.code: - name: Record Deployment (wrong placement) run: | curl -X POST "$METRICS_ENDPOINT/events/deployment" ... ./deploy.sh # deploy happens AFTER recording — wrong

example.note: Deployment Frequency: deployments per day over last 7 days.
Change Failure Rate as percentage over 30 days.
MTTR in minutes (average incident resolution time).
Python function computing lead time statistics from deployment records.

text.reference: Complete GitHub Actions workflow with deployment event emission
Prometheus recording rules and alerting rules for all four metrics
PostgreSQL schema with materialized view for daily DORA aggregation
